input.question:
The above data have been examined and appear to be normally distributed. Use a $t$ test on the data, and record the following:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline & Rural & Urban \\
\hline Mean suspended sediment & & \\
\hline Standard deviation & & \\
\hline $t$ value & & \\
\hline df & & \\
\hline Probability & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

output.answer:
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\hline & Rural & Urban \\
\hline Mean suspended sediment & $55.2 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{1}^{-l}$ & $75.5 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{1}^{-l}$ \\
Standard deviation & $17.58 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{1}^{-l}$ & $21.28 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{1}^{-l}$ \\
$t=2.326$ & $\mathrm{df}=18$ & $P<0.05(=0.0319)$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
